final_project_code

Angelina A., Heonmi K., Caroline S., and Zilin W.

Spring 2026

1. Reading in the data

read_data <- read_excel("./stat571B_reading_data.xlsx")

read_data['total_time'] = read_data['Time (minutes)']*60 + read_data['Time (seconds)']

read_data['format'] = c("Laptop", "Laptop", "Print", 
                        "Laptop", "Print", "Print",
                        "Print", "Print", "Laptop", 
                        "Print", "Laptop", "Laptop",
                        "Laptop", "Print", "Print", 
                        "Laptop", "Print", "Laptop",
                        "Print", "Laptop", "Laptop",
                        "Print", "Laptop", "Print",
                        "Laptop", "Print", "Laptop",
                        "Laptop", "Laptop", "Print",
                        "Print", "Laptop", "Print",
                        "Print", "Print", "Laptop")

read_data["music"] = c("no", "yes", "no", 
                       "yes", "no", "yes", 
                       "no", "yes", "no", 
                       "yes", "no", "yes", 
                       "no", "no", "yes",
                       "yes", "yes", "no", 
                       "no", "no", "yes", 
                       "yes", "yes", "no", 
                       "no", "yes", "yes",
                       "yes", "no", "no",
                       "no", "yes", "yes",
                       "yes", "no", "no")
                
read_data
## # A tibble: 36 × 8
##    Participant  Text `Comprehension score (%)` `Time (minutes)` `Time (seconds)`
##    <chr>       <dbl>                     <dbl>            <dbl>            <dbl>
##  1 P1              1                       100                2               17
##  2 P1              2                        75                2               45
##  3 P1              3                        75                1               58
##  4 P2              1                        75                3               14
##  5 P2              2                        50                2               36
##  6 P2              3                        75                2               51
##  7 P3              1                        75                2                5
##  8 P3              2                       100                2               28
##  9 P3              3                        75                1               49
## 10 P4              1                        75                2               22
## # ℹ 26 more rows
## # ℹ 3 more variables: total_time <dbl>, format <chr>, music <chr>

2. Analysis

We used ANOVA with participant as a blocking factor as required. Additionally, a linear mixed-effects model with participant as a random effect was fitted to account for within-subject correlation, yielding consistent results.

Model 1: Time

# ANOVA for Reading Time
model_time <- lm(total_time ~ format + music + format:music + Participant, data = read_data)
Anova(model_time, type = "III")
## Anova Table (Type III tests)
## 
## Response: total_time
##              Sum Sq Df F value    Pr(>F)    
## (Intercept)   44372  1 88.7429 5.455e-09 ***
## format          286  1  0.5723 0.4577359    
## music          1688  1  3.3756 0.0803612 .  
## Participant   35792 11  6.5075 0.0001274 ***
## format:music     27  1  0.0538 0.8188619    
## Residuals     10500 21                      
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## Mixed Effects Model (Supplementary Analysis)
lmer_time <- lmer(total_time ~ format * music + (1 | Participant), data = read_data)
anova(lmer_time)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## format        354.5   354.5     1 21.899  0.7109 0.408266   
## music        4031.3  4031.3     1 21.899  8.0831 0.009488 **
## format:music   18.0    18.0     1 21.899  0.0362 0.850913   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

For reading time, neither format nor the interaction between format and music was significant. Music showed a marginal effect in the ANOVA model and was significant in the mixed-effects model, suggesting a potential influence of music on reading time after accounting for within-subject variability.

Model 2: Comprehension

# ANOVA for Comprehension
model_comp <- lm(`Comprehension score (%)` ~ format + music + format:music + Participant, data = read_data)
Anova(model_comp, type = "III")
## Anova Table (Type III tests)
## 
## Response: Comprehension score (%)
##               Sum Sq Df F value    Pr(>F)    
## (Intercept)  20782.4  1 81.6182 1.112e-08 ***
## format         850.7  1  3.3409   0.08182 .  
## music          850.7  1  3.3409   0.08182 .  
## Participant   2986.1 11  1.0661   0.43039    
## format:music  1250.0  1  4.9091   0.03790 *  
## Residuals     5347.2 21                      
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## Mixed Effects Model (Supplementary Analysis)
lmer_comp <- lmer(`Comprehension score (%)` ~ format * music + (1 | Participant), data = read_data)
anova(lmer_comp)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value  Pr(>F)  
## format         66.4    66.4     1 25.777  0.2623 0.61289  
## music           0.2     0.2     1 25.777  0.0008 0.97795  
## format:music 1121.0  1121.0     1 25.777  4.4289 0.04524 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

For comprehension, the interaction between format and music was statistically significant, indicating that the effect of format depends on the presence of music. Neither main effect was significant on its own.

Diagnostics for ANOVA

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(model_time)

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(model_comp)

Diagnostic plots indicate no major violations of model assumptions.
